# occCite

## Summary

The `occCite` workflow follows a three-step process. First, the user
inputs one or more taxonomic names (or a phylogeny). `occCite` then
rectifies these names by checking them against one or more taxonomic
databases, which can be specified by the user (see the [Global Names
List](http://gni.globalnames.org/data_sources)). The results of the
taxonomic rectification are then kept in an `occCiteData` object in
local memory. Next, `occCite` takes the `occCiteData` object and
user-defined search parameters to query BIEN (through `rbien`) and/or
GBIF(through `rGBIF`) for records. The results are appended to the
`occCiteData` object, along with metadata on the search. Finally, the
user can pass the `occCiteData` object to `occCitation`, which compiles
citations for the primary providers, database aggregators, and `R`
packages used to build the dataset.

Please cite occCite. Run the following to get the appropriate citation
for the version you’re using:

``` r

citation(package = "occCite")
```

``` R
## 
## Owens H, Merow C, Maitner B, Kass J, Barve V, Guralnick R (2025).
## _occCite: Querying and Managing Large Biodiversity Occurrence
## Datasets_. doi: 10.5281/zenodo.4726676 (URL:
## https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.4726676), R package version 0.6.2,
## <URL: https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=occCite>.
## 
## A BibTeX entry for LaTeX users is
## 
##   @Manual{,
##     title = {occCite: Querying and Managing Large Biodiversity Occurrence Datasets},
##     author = {Hannah Owens and Cory Merow and Brian Maitner and Jamie Kass and Vijay Barve and Robert Guralnick},
##     year = {2025},
##     note = {R package version 0.6.2},
##     url = {https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=occCite},
##     doi = {10.5281/zenodo.4726676},
##   }
```

## Installation:

``` r

install.packages("occCite")
```

Or, install via `r-universe`

``` r

install.packages("occCite", repos = "https://ropensci.r-universe.dev")
```

Or, install github development version:

``` r

devtools::install_github("ropensci/occCite")
```

After using one of these options, you can load the package into your
environment using:

``` r

library("occCite")
```

## Getting Started

- occCite introduction vignette
  (<https://ropensci.github.io/occCite/articles/a_Simple.html>)
- occCite advanced feature vignette
  (<https://ropensci.github.io/occCite/articles/b_Advanced.html>)
- Function reference
  <https://ropensci.github.io/occCite/reference/index.html>
- YouTube tutorial (<https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7qSCULN_VjY&t=17s>)
- Software note in *Ecography* (<https://doi.org/10.1111/ecog.05618>)

## Meta

- Please adhere to the [ROpenSci code of
  conduct](https://ropensci.org/code-of-conduct/).
- Please [report any issues or
  bugs](https://github.com/ropensci/occCite/issues).
- We encourage you to contribute to `occCite`! See the [contributor
  guide](https://ropensci.github.io/occCite/CONTRIBUTING.html) for more
  information.
- License: GPL-3
- Get citation information for `occCite` in R using
  `citation(package = 'occCite')`

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Package index

## All functions

- [`GBIFLogin-class`](https://docs.ropensci.org/occCite/reference/GBIFLogin-class.md)
  [`GBIFLogin`](https://docs.ropensci.org/occCite/reference/GBIFLogin-class.md)
  : GBIFLogin Data Class
- [`GBIFLoginManager()`](https://docs.ropensci.org/occCite/reference/GBIFLoginManager.md)
  : GBIF Login Manager
- [`getBIENpoints()`](https://docs.ropensci.org/occCite/reference/getBIENpoints.md)
  : Download occurrence points from BIEN
- [`getGBIFpoints()`](https://docs.ropensci.org/occCite/reference/getGBIFpoints.md)
  : Download occurrences from GBIF
- [`myOccCiteObject`](https://docs.ropensci.org/occCite/reference/myOccCiteObject.md)
  : Results of an occCite search for \*Protea cynaroides\*
- [`occCitation()`](https://docs.ropensci.org/occCite/reference/occCitation.md)
  : Occurrence Citations
- [`occCiteCitation-class`](https://docs.ropensci.org/occCite/reference/occCiteCitation-class.md)
  [`occCiteCitation`](https://docs.ropensci.org/occCite/reference/occCiteCitation-class.md)
  : occCite Citation Class
- [`occCiteData-class`](https://docs.ropensci.org/occCite/reference/occCiteData-class.md)
  [`occCiteData`](https://docs.ropensci.org/occCite/reference/occCiteData-class.md)
  : occCite Data Class
- [`occCiteMap()`](https://docs.ropensci.org/occCite/reference/occCiteMap.md)
  : Generating a map of downloaded points
- [`occQuery()`](https://docs.ropensci.org/occCite/reference/occQuery.md)
  : Query from Taxon List
- [`plot(`*`<occCiteData>`*`)`](https://docs.ropensci.org/occCite/reference/plot.occCiteData.md)
  : Plotting summary figures for occCite search results
- [`prevGBIFdownload()`](https://docs.ropensci.org/occCite/reference/prevGBIFdownload.md)
  : Download previously-prepared GBIF data sets
- [`print(`*`<occCiteCitation>`*`)`](https://docs.ropensci.org/occCite/reference/print.occCiteCitation.md)
  : Print occCite citation object
- [`studyTaxonList()`](https://docs.ropensci.org/occCite/reference/studyTaxonList.md)
  : Study Taxon List
- [`summary(`*`<occCiteData>`*`)`](https://docs.ropensci.org/occCite/reference/summary.occCiteData.md)
  : Summary for occCite data objects
- [`taxonRectification()`](https://docs.ropensci.org/occCite/reference/taxonRectification.md)
  : Taxon Rectification
